sentences.sentence: Volcanoes are the ultimate earth-moving machinery.
sentences.translation: 火山是最强大的改造地貌的自然机器。
difficult_points: ultimate 强调火山在改变地表方面达到“最强、终极”的程度。
earth-moving machinery 是比喻说法，把火山比作能搬动地壳和地貌的机器。

expressions.meaning_zh: 移动土石的；改造地貌的

sentences.sentence: A violent eruption can blow the top few kilometres off a mountain, scatter fine ash practically all over the globe and hurl rock fragments into the stratosphere to darken the skies a continent away.
sentences.translation: 一次猛烈喷发可以把山顶几公里的部分炸飞，把细灰几乎撒遍全球，并把岩石碎片抛入平流层，使远在另一片大陆的天空变暗。

structure.text: can blow the top few kilometres off a mountain
scatter fine ash practically all over the globe
and hurl rock fragments into the stratosphere
to darken the skies a continent away

difficult_points: can 后连续连接 blow、scatter 和 hurl 三个并列动作，共同说明喷发的破坏力。
off a mountain 表示从山体上被炸离，不是“关掉山”。
to darken the skies a continent away 是结果或目的状语，说明岩石碎片进入平流层后的影响范围。

sentences.sentence: But the classic eruption - cone-shaped mountain, big bang, mushroom cloud and surges of molten lava - is only a tiny part of a global story.
sentences.translation: 但是，人们熟悉的那种喷发景象——锥形山体、巨大爆炸、蘑菇云和汹涌的熔岩——只是一个全球性故事中的很小一部分。

structure.text: cone-shaped mountain, big bang, mushroom cloud and surges of molten lava
only a tiny part of a global story

difficult_points: 破折号之间的并列名词短语解释 the classic eruption 的典型画面。
only a tiny part 表明作者要把话题从单次喷发扩大到全球地质过程。
a global story 指火山活动对整个地球形成过程的影响。

expressions.meaning_zh: 全球范围的过程或背景

sentences.sentence: Volcanism, the name given to volcanic processes, really has shaped the world.
sentences.translation: 火山作用，也就是火山过程的名称，确实塑造了这个世界。

structure.text: the name given to volcanic processes

difficult_points: the name given to volcanic processes 是同位语，解释 Volcanism 的含义。
given to 是过去分词短语，表示“被赋予给……的名称”。
has shaped 使用现在完成时，强调火山作用对现在世界格局仍有影响。

sentences.sentence: Eruptions have rifted continents, raised mountain chains, constructed islands and shaped the topography of the earth.
sentences.translation: 火山喷发曾使大陆裂开、抬升山脉、形成岛屿，并塑造了地球的地形。

structure.text: constructed islands and shaped the topography of the earth

difficult_points: have 后接四个并列过去分词，列举喷发造成的地质结果。
rifted continents 指使大陆发生裂谷或分裂，不是简单“漂移”。
the topography of the earth 指地表地形和起伏格局。

sentences.sentence: The entire ocean floor has a basement of volcanic basalt.
sentences.translation: 整个海底都有一层由火山玄武岩构成的基底。
difficult_points: entire 强调海底范围是整体性的，而非局部区域。
basement 在地质语境中指基底或底层，不是建筑物地下室。
of volcanic basalt 说明 basement 的材料来源。

sentences.sentence: Volcanoes have not only made the continents, they are also thought to have made the world's first stable atmosphere and provided all the water for the oceans, rivers and ice-caps.
sentences.translation: 火山不仅造就了大陆，人们还认为它们形成了世界上最早的稳定大气，并为海洋、河流和冰盖提供了全部水分。

structure.text: Volcanoes have not only made the continents
to have made the world's first stable atmosphere
and provided all the water for the oceans, rivers and ice-caps

difficult_points: not only 与 also 呼应，强调火山的作用不止形成大陆。
are thought to have made 是被动表达，表示“被认为已经形成”。
provided all the water 后的 for the oceans, rivers and ice-caps 说明水最终进入的对象。

sentences.sentence: There are now about 600 active volcanoes.
sentences.translation: 目前大约有600座活火山。
difficult_points: There are 是存在句，真正的信息焦点是 about 600 active volcanoes。
active volcanoes 指仍可能喷发或正在活动的火山。

sentences.sentence: Every year they add two or three cubic kilometres of rock to the continents.
sentences.translation: 每年，它们都会给大陆增加两到三立方公里的岩石。

structure.text: add two or three cubic kilometres of rock to the continents

difficult_points: they 回指上一句中的 active volcanoes。
add ... to ... 表示把岩石量不断加入大陆，强调累积过程。
cubic kilometres 是体积单位，说明增加的是岩石体积而非面积。

sentences.sentence: Imagine a similar number of volcanoes smoking away for the last 3,500 million years.
sentences.translation: 想象一下，数量差不多的火山在过去35亿年里一直喷着烟。

structure.text: smoking away for the last 3,500 million years

difficult_points: Imagine 是祈使句，引导读者进行时间尺度上的估算。
smoking away 是现在分词短语，描写 volcanoes 长期持续活动的状态。
3,500 million years 等于35亿年，表示极长的地质时间。

sentences.sentence: That is enough rock to explain the continental crust.
sentences.translation: 这些岩石的数量足以解释大陆地壳的形成。
difficult_points: That 回指前一句假设中长期累积的火山岩总量。
enough ... to ... 表示数量达到可以解释某种结果的程度。
continental crust 指大陆地壳，不是整个地球外壳。

sentences.sentence: What comes out of volcanic craters is mostly gas.
sentences.translation: 从火山口喷出的物质大多是气体。

structure.text: What comes out of volcanic craters

difficult_points: What 引导主语从句，整体表示“从火山口出来的东西”。
comes out of 是短语动词，指喷出或出来。
mostly gas 表明作者从岩石转向解释火山气体的作用。

sentences.sentence: More than 90% of this gas is water vapour from the deep earth: enough to explain, over 3,500 million years, the water in the oceans.
sentences.translation: 这些气体中超过90%是来自地球深处的水蒸气；经过35亿年的积累，这足以解释海洋中的水。

structure.text: is water vapour from the deep earth

difficult_points: 冒号后的 enough to explain 省略了 it is，补充说明前面水蒸气数量的意义。
from the deep earth 修饰 water vapour，说明水蒸气来源于地球深处。
over 3,500 million years 是时间跨度状语，强调长期累积。

sentences.sentence: The rest of the gas is nitrogen, carbon dioxide, sulphur dioxide, methane, ammonia and hydrogen.
sentences.translation: 其余气体则是氮气、二氧化碳、二氧化硫、甲烷、氨和氢气。

structure.text: nitrogen, carbon dioxide, sulphur dioxide, methane, ammonia and hydrogen

difficult_points: The rest of the gas 指除水蒸气以外的火山气体。
后面一串名词并列列出气体成分，要整体理解为表语。
sulphur dioxide 使用英式拼写 sulphur。

sentences.sentence: The quantity of these gases, again multiplied over 3,500 million years, is enough to explain the mass of the world's atmosphere.
sentences.translation: 这些气体的数量如果同样乘以35亿年的时间，就足以解释地球大气的总量。

structure.text: the mass of the world's atmosphere

difficult_points: again multiplied over 3,500 million years 是插入的过去分词短语，说明计算方式。
is enough to explain 延续前一句的逻辑：长期累积的数量可以解释宏观结果。
the mass of the world's atmosphere 指全球大气总量。

sentences.sentence: We are alive because volcanoes provided the soil, air and water we need.
sentences.translation: 我们之所以能活着，是因为火山提供了我们所需的土壤、空气和水。

structure.text: because volcanoes provided the soil, air and water

difficult_points: because 引导原因状语从句，解释人类能够生存的根本条件。
we need 是省略关系代词 that 的定语从句，修饰 the soil, air and water。
provided 在这里强调火山长期地质作用带来的生命条件。

sentences.sentence: Geologists consider the earth as having a molten core, surrounded by a semi-molten mantle and a brittle, outer skin.
sentences.translation: 地质学家认为地球有一个熔融的核心，外面包裹着半熔融的地幔和一层脆硬的外壳。

structure.text: consider the earth as having a molten core
surrounded by a semi-molten mantle and a brittle, outer skin

difficult_points: consider ... as having ... 表示“把……看作具有……”。
surrounded by 是过去分词短语，补充说明 molten core 外面的层次。
outer skin 是比喻说法，指地球外层坚硬而易碎的地壳。

sentences.sentence: It helps to think of a soft-boiled egg with a runny yolk, a firm but squishy white and a hard shell.
sentences.translation: 把它想象成一个溏心蛋会有帮助：里面是流动的蛋黄，外面是较硬但仍柔软的蛋白和坚硬的蛋壳。

structure.text: to think of a soft-boiled egg
with a runny yolk, a firm but squishy white and a hard shell

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正的内容是后面的 to think of ...。
soft-boiled egg 的结构被用来类比地球内部层次。
with 短语列出蛋黄、蛋白和蛋壳，对应地核、地幔和地壳。

sentences.sentence: If the shell is even slightly cracked during boiling, the white material bubbles out and sets like a tiny mountain chain over the crack - like an archipelago of volcanic islands such as the Hawaiian Islands.
sentences.translation: 如果蛋壳在煮的过程中哪怕只是微微裂开，蛋白也会冒出来，并在裂缝上凝固成一小条山脉般的形状——就像由夏威夷群岛这样的火山岛组成的群岛。

structure.text: If the shell is even slightly cracked during boiling
and sets like a tiny mountain chain over the crack
like an archipelago of volcanic islands such as the Hawaiian Islands

difficult_points: If 引导条件状语从句，说明蛋壳裂开时会出现的现象。
bubbles out and sets 是两个连续动作：冒出并凝固。
破折号后的 like 短语把凝固的蛋白比作火山岛链。

sentences.sentence: But the earth is so much bigger and the mantle below is so much hotter.
sentences.translation: 但地球要大得多，下面的地幔也热得多。

structure.text: the earth is so much bigger
and the mantle below is so much hotter

difficult_points: But 表示从鸡蛋类比转回真实地球时的对比。
so much bigger 和 so much hotter 都用 much 强化比较级。
the mantle below 指地壳下方的地幔。

sentences.sentence: Even though the mantle rocks are kept solid by overlying pressure, they can still slowly "flow" like thick treacle.
sentences.translation: 尽管地幔岩石在上覆压力下保持固态，它们仍能像浓糖浆一样缓慢“流动”。

structure.text: Even though the mantle rocks are kept solid by overlying pressure

difficult_points: Even though 引导让步状语从句，突出“固态”与“流动”之间的反差。
are kept solid 是被动结构，说明压力使岩石保持固态。
"flow" 加引号表明这是非常缓慢、近似流动的地质过程。

sentences.sentence: The flow, thought to be in the form of convection currents, is powerful enough to fracture the "eggshell" of the crust into plates, and keep them bumping and grinding against each other, or even overlapping, at the rate of a few centimetres a year.
sentences.translation: 这种流动被认为以对流的形式存在，其力量足以把地壳这层“蛋壳”断裂成板块，并使这些板块彼此碰撞、摩擦，甚至重叠，速度约为每年几厘米。

structure.text: thought to be in the form of convection currents
is powerful enough to fracture the "eggshell" of the crust into plates
and keep them bumping and grinding against each other
at the rate of a few centimetres a year

difficult_points: thought to be ... 是过去分词插入语，说明这种流动的形式。
powerful enough to fracture ... and keep ... 中 enough to 后面包含两个结果动作。
them 指前面的 plates，说明板块之间发生碰撞、摩擦甚至重叠。
at the rate of 表示变化速度，而不是地点。

sentences.sentence: These fracture zones, where the collisions occur, are where earthquakes happen.
sentences.translation: 这些断裂带，也就是碰撞发生的地方，正是地震发生的地方。
difficult_points: where the collisions occur 是非限制性定语从句，补充说明 fracture zones。
are where ... 用地点从句作表语，表示“正是……发生之处”。
collisions 回指上一句板块之间的碰撞和摩擦。

sentences.sentence: And, very often, volcanoes.
sentences.translation: 而且，这些地方也常常会出现火山。
difficult_points: 这是省略句，完整意思是这些断裂带也常常是火山发生或出现的地方。
very often 作插入状语，强调火山与地震断裂带经常相伴。
And 连接上一句，使 volcanoes 与 earthquakes 并列。

sentences.sentence: These zones are lines of weakness, or hot spots.
sentences.translation: 这些区域是薄弱线，也就是热点。

structure.text: lines of weakness, or hot spots

difficult_points: These zones 回指上一段的 fracture zones。
or hot spots 用 or 作解释性连接，说明这些薄弱区域也可称为热点。
lines of weakness 是地质意义上的薄弱线。

sentences.sentence: Every eruption is different, but put at its simplest, where there are weaknesses, rocks deep in the mantle, heated to 1,350°C, will start to expand and rise.
sentences.translation: 每次喷发都不相同，但最简单地说，在存在薄弱处的地方，地幔深处被加热到1350摄氏度的岩石会开始膨胀并上升。

structure.text: will start to expand and rise

difficult_points: but 后主干是 rocks ... will start to expand and rise，中间插入多个状语和修饰语。
put at its simplest 是固定插入表达，表示“最简单地说”。
heated to 1,350°C 是过去分词短语，修饰 rocks。
where there are weaknesses 表示岩石上升开始的地质位置。

sentences.sentence: As they do so, the pressure drops, and they expand and become liquid and rise more swiftly.
sentences.translation: 当它们这样上升时，压力会下降，于是它们膨胀、变成液态，并更快地上升。

structure.text: and they expand and become liquid and rise more swiftly

difficult_points: As 引导时间状语从句，do so 代替前一句的 expand and rise。
drops、expand、become 和 rise 构成连锁过程，说明压力下降后的变化。
they 指代前一句中的 rocks。

sentences.sentence: Sometimes it is slow: vast bubbles of magma - molten rock from the mantle - inch towards the surface, cooling slowly, to show through as granite extrusions (as on Skye, or the Great Whin Sill, the lava dyke squeezed out like toothpaste that carries part of Hadrian's Wall in northern England).
sentences.translation: 有时这个过程很慢：巨大的岩浆泡——来自地幔的熔融岩石——会一点点向地表移动，缓慢冷却，最后以花岗岩侵入体的形式露出来（就像斯凯岛或大惠因岩床那样，那里像牙膏一样被挤出的熔岩岩脉承载着英格兰北部哈德良长城的一部分）。

structure.text: to show through as granite extrusions
that carries part of Hadrian's Wall in northern England

difficult_points: 冒号后解释 slow 的具体过程，主语是 vast bubbles of magma。
破折号中的 molten rock from the mantle 是 magma 的同位解释。
cooling slowly 和 to show through 分别说明移动过程中的状态和最终结果。
括号内 as on Skye ... 提供具体地理例子，其中 that 引导定语从句修饰 the lava dyke。

sentences.sentence: Sometimes - as in Northern Ireland, Wales and the Karoo in South Africa - the magma rose faster, and then flowed out horizontally on to the surface in vast thick sheets.
sentences.translation: 有时——如在北爱尔兰、威尔士和南非的卡鲁地区——岩浆上升得更快，然后以大片厚层的形式水平流到地表。

structure.text: as in Northern Ireland, Wales and the Karoo in South Africa
and then flowed out horizontally on to the surface

difficult_points: 破折号之间的 as in ... 是插入例子，说明这种喷发类型出现的地区。
rose faster 和 flowed out 是按时间顺序排列的两个动作。
on to the surface 表示流动到地表，in vast thick sheets 描述熔岩铺展形态。

sentences.sentence: In the Deccan plateau in western India, there are more than two million cubic kilometres of lava, some of it 2,400 metres thick, formed over 500,000 years of slurping eruption.
sentences.translation: 在印度西部的德干高原，有超过两百万立方公里的熔岩，其中一些厚达2400米，是在50万年的汩汩喷发中形成的。

structure.text: In the Deccan plateau in western India
there are more than two million cubic kilometres of lava
formed over 500,000 years of slurping eruption

difficult_points: there are 构成存在句，地点状语放在句首。
some of it 2,400 metres thick 是独立结构，说明部分熔岩层的厚度。
formed over ... 是过去分词短语，说明这些熔岩形成所经历的时间。
slurping eruption 形象地描写熔岩持续涌出的喷发。

sentences.sentence: Sometimes the magma moves very swiftly indeed.
sentences.translation: 有时岩浆移动得确实非常迅速。
difficult_points: Sometimes 与前面两种较慢情况形成对比，引出快速喷发类型。
indeed 加强 very swiftly，强调速度非常快。
the magma 延续前文岩浆上升与流动的主题。

sentences.sentence: It does not have time to cool as it surges upwards.
sentences.translation: 它在向上急速涌动时没有时间冷却。

structure.text: does not have time to cool

difficult_points: It 指代上一句的 the magma。
have time to cool 表示来得及冷却，否定形式说明速度太快。
as 引导时间状语从句，说明冷却不足发生在岩浆上涌过程中。

sentences.sentence: The gases trapped inside the boiling rock expand suddenly, the lava glows with heat, it begins to froth, and it explodes with tremendous force.
sentences.translation: 被困在沸腾岩石内部的气体突然膨胀，熔岩因高温而发光，开始起泡，随后以巨大的力量爆炸。

structure.text: The gases trapped inside the boiling rock expand suddenly
and it explodes with tremendous force

difficult_points: trapped inside the boiling rock 是过去分词短语，修饰 gases。
句中逗号连接多个连续事件，呈现爆炸发生前后的过程链。
it 在后半句指代 the lava。
with tremendous force 表示爆炸的力量大小。

sentences.sentence: Then the slightly cooler lava following it begins to flow over the lip of the crater.
sentences.translation: 随后，跟在后面、稍微冷却一些的熔岩开始越过火山口边缘流出。

structure.text: the slightly cooler lava following it
begins to flow over the lip of the crater

difficult_points: following it 是现在分词短语，修饰 lava，表示后续涌出的熔岩。
it 回指前一句中爆炸的熔岩。
the lip of the crater 指火山口边缘，lip 是比喻用法。

sentences.sentence: It happens on Mars, it happened on the moon, it even happens on some of the moons of Jupiter and Uranus.
sentences.translation: 这种现象发生在火星上，曾发生在月球上，甚至也发生在木星和天王星的一些卫星上。

structure.text: it even happens on some of the moons of Jupiter and Uranus

difficult_points: It 指代前文描述的火山喷发过程。
happens、happened、happens 的时态变化区分火星、月球和其他卫星上的发生情况。
even 强调这种火山现象并不局限于地球或月球。

sentences.sentence: By studying the evidence, volcanologists can read the force of the great blasts of the past.
sentences.translation: 通过研究证据，火山学家能够判断过去大型爆发的力量。

structure.text: can read the force of the great blasts of the past

difficult_points: By studying the evidence 表示手段，说明火山学家如何判断过去喷发。
read 在这里不是普通“阅读”，而是“解读、判断”。
of the past 修饰 great blasts，指过去发生的大喷发。

sentences.sentence: Is the pumice light and full of holes?
sentences.translation: 浮石是不是很轻并且布满孔洞？
difficult_points: 这是一般疑问句，作者用问答方式说明证据如何对应喷发强度。
light and full of holes 描述浮石的物理特征。
pumice 是火山喷发形成的多孔轻质岩石。

sentences.sentence: The explosion was tremendous.
sentences.translation: 那次爆炸就非常猛烈。
difficult_points: The explosion 回答上一句的证据推断：轻且多孔说明爆炸强烈。
tremendous 表示程度很大，强调喷发能量巨大。
简短句子用于加强结论感。

sentences.sentence: Are the rocks heavy, with huge crystalline basalt shapes, like the Giant's Causeway in Northern Ireland?
sentences.translation: 岩石是不是很重，并带有巨大的结晶玄武岩形态，就像北爱尔兰的巨人堤道那样？

structure.text: like the Giant's Causeway in Northern Ireland

difficult_points: 这是第二个证据问题，与上一组轻质浮石形成对比。
with huge crystalline basalt shapes 补充岩石的形态特征。
like the Giant's Causeway in Northern Ireland 给出具体类比。

sentences.sentence: It was a slow, gentle eruption.
sentences.translation: 那就是一次缓慢而温和的喷发。
difficult_points: It 指代由上一句岩石特征所反推的喷发。
slow, gentle 与前面的 tremendous explosion 形成对照。
短句直接给出根据岩石证据得出的结论。

sentences.sentence: The biggest eruptions are deep on the mid-ocean floor, where new lava is forcing the continents apart and widening the Atlantic by perhaps five centimetres a year.
sentences.translation: 规模最大的喷发发生在大洋中脊深处，在那里新的熔岩正把大陆推开，并以每年大约五厘米的速度拓宽大西洋。

structure.text: are deep on the mid-ocean floor
where new lava is forcing the continents apart and widening the Atlantic
by perhaps five centimetres a year

difficult_points: where 引导非限制性定语从句，说明 mid-ocean floor 处发生的作用。
forcing ... apart and widening ... 是两个并列现在分词动作。
by perhaps five centimetres a year 表示大西洋每年的拓宽幅度。

sentences.sentence: Look at maps of volcanoes, earthquakes and island chains like the Philippines and Japan, and you can see the rough outlines of what are called tectonic plates - the plates which make up the earth's crust and mantle.
sentences.translation: 看看火山、地震和像菲律宾、日本这样的岛链分布图，你就能看到所谓构造板块的大致轮廓——这些板块构成了地壳和地幔。

structure.text: Look at maps of volcanoes, earthquakes and island chains
and you can see the rough outlines
of what are called tectonic plates
the plates which make up the earth's crust and mantle
which make up the earth's crust and mantle

difficult_points: Look at ... and you can see ... 是祈使句加并列句，表示“如果看……就能看到……”。
what are called tectonic plates 是 what 引导的名词性结构，作 of 的宾语。
破折号后的 the plates 是对 tectonic plates 的同位解释。
which make up ... 是定语从句，说明板块构成地壳和地幔。

sentences.sentence: The most dramatic of these is the Pacific "ring of fire" where there have been the most violent explosions - Mount Pinatubo near Manila, Mount St Helens in the Rockies and El Chichón in Mexico about a decade ago, not to mention world-shaking blasts like Krakatoa in the Sunda Straits in 1883.
sentences.translation: 其中最引人注目的是太平洋“火环”，那里曾发生过最猛烈的爆炸——比如马尼拉附近的皮纳图博火山、落基山脉中的圣海伦斯火山，以及大约十年前墨西哥的埃尔奇琼火山，更不用说1883年巽他海峡中像喀拉喀托那样震动世界的大爆发了。

structure.text: where there have been the most violent explosions
Mount Pinatubo near Manila, Mount St Helens in the Rockies and El Chichón in Mexico about a decade ago
not to mention world-shaking blasts like Krakatoa in the Sunda Straits in 1883

difficult_points: where 引导定语从句，说明 Pacific "ring of fire" 是猛烈爆发发生的地区。
破折号后的三个火山名是 violent explosions 的例子。
not to mention 用来追加更强的例子，强调喀拉喀托爆发的全球影响。
about a decade ago 修饰 El Chichón in Mexico 的时间背景。

sentences.sentence: But volcanoes are not very predictable.
sentences.translation: 但是，火山并不是很容易预测。
difficult_points: But 标志话题从火山机制转向火山预测难度。
not very predictable 是缓和表达，表示火山活动难以准确预判。
predictable 是形容词作表语。

sentences.sentence: That is because geological time is not like human time.
sentences.translation: 这是因为地质时间不同于人类时间。

structure.text: is because geological time is not like human time
geological time is not like human time

difficult_points: That 回指上一句火山难以预测这一判断。
because 后解释原因：地质过程的时间尺度与人类经验不同。
not like human time 强调预测困难来自时间尺度差异。

sentences.sentence: During quiet periods, volcanoes cap themselves with their own lava by forming a powerful cone from the molten rocks slopping over the rim of the crater; later the lava cools slowly into a huge, hard, stable plug which blocks any further eruption until the pressure below becomes irresistible.
sentences.translation: 在平静期，火山通过由溢出火山口边缘的熔融岩石形成坚固的锥体，用自己的熔岩把自身封住；随后熔岩缓慢冷却成一个巨大、坚硬、稳定的栓塞，阻挡进一步喷发，直到下方压力变得不可抗拒。

structure.text: volcanoes cap themselves with their own lava
from the molten rocks slopping over the rim of the crater
later the lava cools slowly into a huge, hard, stable plug
until the pressure below becomes irresistible

difficult_points: 分号连接两个阶段：火山先封住自己，随后熔岩冷却形成堵塞。
by forming ... 表示火山封住自身的方式。
slopping over the rim of the crater 是现在分词短语，修饰 molten rocks。
which 引导定语从句修饰 plug，until 从句说明堵塞持续到压力无法抵挡时。

sentences.sentence: In the case of Mount Pinatubo, this took 600 years.
sentences.translation: 就皮纳图博火山而言，这个过程用了600年。

structure.text: In the case of Mount Pinatubo

difficult_points: In the case of 用于引入具体案例。
this 回指上一句中封住、冷却并积累压力的过程。
took 600 years 强调火山时间尺度远超人类日常经验。

sentences.sentence: Then, sometimes, with only a small warning, the mountain blows its top.
sentences.translation: 然后，有时只出现一点点预警，火山就会爆发。
difficult_points: Then 承接长时间积累压力后的突然变化。
with only a small warning 表示爆发前预警很少。
blows its top 是习语化表达，字面是“掀掉顶部”，这里指火山爆发。

sentences.sentence: It did this at Mont Pelée in Martinique at 7.49 a.m. on 8 May, 1902.
sentences.translation: 1902年5月8日上午7点49分，马提尼克岛的培雷火山就发生了这样的爆发。
difficult_points: It 指代前一句的 the mountain，即具体案例中的火山。
did this 回指 blows its top，表示发生了突然爆发。
两个 at 短语分别给出地点和精确时间，a.m. 不是句末结束。

expressions.meaning_zh: 做了这件事；发生了这种情况

sentences.sentence: Of a town of 28,000, only two people survived.
sentences.translation: 在一个有28000人的城镇中，只有两个人幸存。
difficult_points: Of a town of 28,000 放在句首，表示幸存者相对于全镇人口的比例。
only 强调幸存人数极少，突出灾难严重性。
survived 是不及物动词，表示从灾难中幸存。

sentences.sentence: In 1815, a sudden blast removed the top 1,280 metres of Mount Tambora in Indonesia.
sentences.translation: 1815年，一次突然的爆发削去了印度尼西亚坦博拉火山顶部1280米。

structure.text: removed the top 1,280 metres of Mount Tambora in Indonesia

difficult_points: In 1815 提供另一个历史案例的时间。
removed the top 1,280 metres 表示火山顶部被爆发直接削掉。
of Mount Tambora in Indonesia 指明被削去的是坦博拉火山的顶部。

sentences.sentence: The eruption was so fierce that dust thrown into the stratosphere darkened the skies, canceling the following summer in Europe and North America.
sentences.translation: 这次喷发如此猛烈，以至于被抛入平流层的尘埃遮暗了天空，使欧洲和北美接下来的那个夏天消失了。

structure.text: that dust thrown into the stratosphere darkened the skies
canceling the following summer in Europe and North America

difficult_points: so ... that ... 构成结果状语结构，说明喷发猛烈到造成后果。
thrown into the stratosphere 是过去分词短语，修饰 dust。
canceling the following summer 是现在分词短语，表示天空变暗带来的结果。
following summer 指喷发后的那个夏天。

sentences.sentence: Thousands starved as the harvest failed, after snow in June and frosts in August.
sentences.translation: 六月降雪、八月霜冻之后，庄稼歉收，成千上万人因此挨饿。

structure.text: after snow in June and frosts in August

difficult_points: Thousands 是省略表达，指成千上万的人。
as 引导原因或伴随状语从句，说明饥荒与歉收相关。
after snow in June and frosts in August 说明异常天气导致收成失败。

sentences.sentence: Volcanoes are potentially world news, especially the quiet ones.
sentences.translation: 火山随时都可能成为世界新闻，尤其是那些看似平静的火山。
difficult_points: potentially 表示火山具备成为重大新闻的可能性，并非一定正在发生。
world news 指影响全球或引起全球关注的事件。
the quiet ones 指看似平静但可能积累压力的火山，与本段预测困难呼应。
